metadata.name: Golgari Grave-Troll dredge replaces draw step draw
metadata.description: P1 has Golgari Grave-Troll in their graveyard. It is P1's draw step (turn 2, so first-turn skip does not apply). P1 would draw a card. Because Golgari Grave-Troll has Dredge 6 and P1's library has 8 cards (>= 6), a DredgeChoiceRequired event is emitted. P1 chooses to dredge: the top 6 cards of the library are milled into the graveyard, and Golgari Grave-Troll returns from graveyard to hand (CR 702.52a). After dredge: P1's hand increases from 2 to 3 cards, library decreases from 8 to 2, graveyard gains 6 milled cards and loses the Grave-Troll. This tests the dredge replacement effect as a WouldDraw replacement (CR 614.1a) that fires during the draw step's turn-based action.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.52a: Dredge N is a static ability that functions only while the card is in a player's graveyard. 'As long as you have at least N cards in your library, if you would draw a card, you may instead mill N cards and return this card from your graveyard to your hand.' This is a WouldDraw replacement effect (CR 614.1a — uses 'instead' language). Golgari Grave-Troll has Dredge 6, so P1 must have >= 6 cards in library for the option to be offered (CR 702.52b). P1 starts with 8 library cards, 2 hand cards, and GGT in graveyard. Draw step (CR 504.1) executes draw_for_turn which calls draw_card which invokes check_would_draw_replacement, which finds GGT in graveyard and emits DredgeChoiceRequired. P1 sends Command::ChooseDredge { player: p1, card: Some(<GGT object id>) }. Engine mills 6 cards (top of library to graveyard), then moves GGT from graveyard to hand. Net result: hand 2->3, library 8->2, graveyard +6 milled -1 GGT = net 5 new cards in graveyard. Dredge does NOT increment cards_drawn_this_turn (CR 702.52a — dredge is a replacement, not a draw). Library cards use Forest (8 copies) which are valid card definitions in the engine. Hand cards use Mountain/Forest to keep the scenario minimal. Harness validation failed after harness run: zones.hand.p1.count expected 3, got 2 (7/9 assertions passed, 2 failed). Root cause: translate_player_action() in replay_harness.rs does not handle 'choose_dredge' — the action falls through to the catch-all branch and returns None, so Command::ChooseDredge is never issued. The pre-dredge assertions (hand count 2, GGT in graveyard, stack empty) all pass, confirming the initial state and DredgeChoiceRequired fire correctly. The post-dredge assertions fail only because the dredge command is never executed. The expected values (hand 3, GGT not in graveyard) are unambiguously correct per CR 702.52a. This is a harness gap, not an engine gap or script logic error — the engine's dredge implementation is verified by 10 dedicated unit tests in crates/engine/tests/dredge.rs. Fix: add 'choose_dredge' to translate_player_action() to find the named card in the player's graveyard and return Command::ChooseDredge { player, card: Some(obj_id) }.

disputes.description: The 'choose_dredge' player action is not supported by translate_player_action() in replay_harness.rs. The function falls through to the '_' catch-all branch and returns None, causing the ChooseDredge command to be silently skipped. As a result, the harness cannot drive the dredge resolution, and the post-dredge assertions (hand count 3, graveyard excludes GGT) fail because the dredge never executes. Harness run result: passed=false, 7/9 assertions, first_failure at step_index=5 path='zones.hand.p1.count' expected=3 actual=2. The pre-dredge assertions (step_index 0,1,2 = 6 individual checks) all passed. Pending harness enhancement: add a 'choose_dredge' branch to translate_player_action that finds the named card in the player's graveyard and returns Command::ChooseDredge { player, card: Some(obj_id) }.
disputes.resolution: RESOLVED (2026-02-26): Added choose_dredge action type to replay_harness.rs translate_player_action(). Script now passes 9/9 assertions.

script.step_note: Draw step begins. Golgari Grave-Troll in graveyard offers Dredge 6.

actions.description: Initial: GGT in p1 graveyard, p1 hand count 2, stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.52a: Golgari Grave-Troll is in the graveyard and P1 has 8 library cards (>= 6), so Dredge 6 will be offered when P1 would draw.

CR 504.1: Active player draws a card during the draw step. CR 702.52a: Because GGT (Dredge 6) is in P1's graveyard and library has >= 6 cards, the engine emits DredgeChoiceRequired instead of performing the draw immediately. The draw is paused awaiting Command::ChooseDredge.

actions.description: After draw step turn-based action fires: hand still 2 (draw paused for dredge choice), GGT still in graveyard, stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.52a: The draw is replaced by a DredgeChoiceRequired event. No card drawn yet. GGT still in graveyard. The engine is paused waiting for ChooseDredge.

CR 702.52a: P1 chooses to dredge Golgari Grave-Troll (Dredge 6). Engine will: (1) mill 6 cards from top of P1's library into graveyard, (2) move GGT from graveyard to hand. This is NOT a draw — cards_drawn_this_turn is not incremented. DISPUTE: choose_dredge is not supported in translate_player_action — this action is silently skipped by the harness.

actions.description: After dredge resolves: p1 hand count 3 (was 2 +1 GGT returned), graveyard does NOT include GGT (it is now in hand), stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.52a: Dredge 6 milled 6 cards (top of library) and returned GGT to hand. Hand: 2 original + 1 GGT = 3. Library: 8 - 6 = 2. GGT is no longer in graveyard (moved to hand). Graveyard gained 6 milled basic lands. Stack remains empty — dredge does not use the stack (CR 614.1: replacement effects apply continuously, not via the stack).
